summarization.a: The figure shows the thickness of WO₃ after exposure to O₂/O₃ and BCl₃/HF as a function of the number of half-cycles.
summarization.b: The figure shows the thickness of W as a function of the number of half-cycles at 207 °C, with O2/O3, BCl3 and HF exposure. The figure shows an etch rate of 2.44 Å/cycle.

data_extraction.a:
| Number of Half-Cycles | WO₃ Thickness (Å) After O₂/O₃ | WO₃ Thickness (Å) After BCl₃/HF |
| --- | --- | --- |
| 0 | 38 | 27 |
| 10 | 43 | 35 |
| 20 | 37 | 30 |
| 30 | 33 | 25 |
| 40 | 30 | 23 |
| 50 | 26 | 20 |
| 60 | 26 | 17 |

b.question: What are the conditions of the experiment?
b.answer: The experiment was performed at 207 °C with the following exposure: 3150 mTorr s of O2/O3, 329 mTorr s of BCl3, and 2800 mTorr s of HF.

b.question: How does the W thickness change with the number of half-cycles?
b.answer: The W thickness decreases as the number of half-cycles increases.

b.question: What is the etch rate reached for this process?
b.answer: As can be seenin the figure, the etch per cycle is 2.44 Å/cycle.

a.question: How does the WO3 thickness change after each exposure?
a.answer: The thickness of the WO3 increases after exposure to O2/O3 because it can oxidize the surface. However, it decreases after exposure to BCl3/HF.
